description: Create the Bellows API client at lib/bellows/client.ts that directly calls the LiteLLM proxy endpoints. Add a BellowsSection to settings for connection (Server URL + API Key) and integrate the budget display and model listing into the settings tab.
steps: Create a new git branch `gate/ax4-bellows-api-client` from the current HEAD.

Create `lib/bellows/client.ts` exporting a Bellows API client module. It should: (a) Define a `BellowsConnectionSettings` type with `serverUrl` (string, default 'http://127.0.0.1:4000') and `apiKey` (string, default 'sk-anvil-safe-key'). (b) Export async functions: `bellowsChatCompletion(settings, params: {model, messages, max_tokens?})` calling POST /v1/chat/completions, `bellowsListModels(settings)` calling GET /model/list, `bellowsAddModel(settings, params)` calling POST /model/add, `bellowsBudgetInfo(settings)` calling GET /budget/info, `bellowsSpendTotal(settings)` calling GET /spend/total, `bellowsSpendLogs(settings)` calling GET /spend/logs, `bellowsCacheInfo(settings)` calling GET /cache/info. Each function uses fetch with Authorization: Bearer {apiKey} header and Accept/Content-Type application/json. Return typed responses. (c) Export a `BellowsBudgetInfo` type: { current_spend: number; max_budget: number | null }. (d) Export a `BellowsModel` type: { id: string; [key: string]: any }.
Create `lib/bellows/types.ts` if needed to keep type definitions separate, or keep them in client.ts if it stays small.

Extend `lib/opencode/client.ts` - add to `OpencodeConnectionSettings` two optional fields: `bellowsServerUrl?: string` and `bellowsApiKey?: string`. Update `defaultConnectionSettings` to include `bellowsServerUrl: 'http://127.0.0.1:4000'` and `bellowsApiKey: 'sk-anvil-safe-key'`.

Create `components/settings/bellows-section.tsx` with a `BellowsSection` component. It receives palette, bellowsServerUrl, bellowsApiKey, updateSettings callback, budget info (nullable), model list (array), loading states, and a refresh callback. Layout: (a) Card titled 'Bellows API' with TextInput for 'Bellows Server URL' (placeholder http://your-bellows:4000) and TextInput for 'API Key' (secureTextEntry). (b) A 'Test Connection' button that triggers refresh. (c) Budget display section: if budget info is available, show 'Spent' (formatted number), 'Limit' (formatted or 'Unlimited'), 'Remaining' (calculated), and a visual progress bar using a View with percentage width and IIG oxidrot/amber coloring from palette.tint. (d) Model listing section: if models array has items, show them as a List with model IDs.

In `app/(tabs)/settings.tsx`: (a) Import `BellowsSection` and the bellows client functions (`bellowsBudgetInfo`, `bellowsListModels`) from `@/lib/bellows/client`. (b) Add state: `bellowsBudget`, `bellowsModels`, `isBellowsLoading`. (c) Add a `refreshBellows` async function that calls bellowsBudgetInfo and bellowsListModels using settings.bellowsServerUrl and settings.bellowsApiKey, updating state. (d) Render `<BellowsSection />` in the ScrollView between ConnectionSection and AiDefaultsSection.
Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` to confirm no errors. Fix any issues found.
acceptance_criteria: lib/bellows/client.ts exists with exported functions for all LiteLLM proxy endpoints (chat completions, model list, model add, budget info, spend total, spend logs, cache info)
BellowsSection renders in the settings tab with server URL and API key inputs, budget display (with progress bar), and model listing
OpencodeConnectionSettings type includes bellowsServerUrl and bellowsApiKey fields
The @opencode-ai/sdk dependency is not removed and lib/opencode/client.ts still works unchanged
`npm run typecheck` passes with zero errors
`npm run lint` passes with zero errors
verification: Run `npm run typecheck` in /projects/sandbox/ANVIL-CLIENT and confirm exit code 0
Run `npm run lint` in /projects/sandbox/ANVIL-CLIENT and confirm exit code 0
findings: Typecheck and lint both pass cleanly. The BellowsSection uses type assertion for percentage-based width styling which TypeScript accepts without issue. The existing @opencode-ai/sdk client remains untouched.
